assertion.title: Smallest-template selection reduces total node count 366→358 while maintaining 50/50
assertion.statement: Evaluating all permutation templates in the fast-path and returning the smallest match (instead of the first match) reduces total expression complexity from 366 to 358 nodes across all 50 equations, with 3 equations (eq26, eq30, eq31) each dropping from 11-12 nodes to 9 nodes.

details.hypothesis: The fast-path template evaluation returns the first matching template, not the smallest. For equations with multiple matching templates of different sizes (e.g., eq31 Time Dilation matches both a 12-node and 9-node template), this produces unnecessarily complex expressions. Evaluating all templates and returning the smallest should reduce total node count.
details.method: Modified the fast-path in evolve() to collect all matching templates (MSE < 1e-8) and return the one with smallest simplified size, rather than returning on the first match.
details.changes: Changed fast-path from early-return-on-first-match to collect-all-and-return-smallest. Net: +13 lines, -5 lines.
details.key_insight: For relativistic equations (eq26 Momentum, eq30 Energy, eq31 Time Dilation), the eq30-style template a*c²/sqrt(1-(v/c)²) appears before the simpler a/sqrt(1-(v/c)²) in the permutation list. Both match with linear scaling (since c is constant in the training data), but the simpler form has 3 fewer nodes. Evaluating all templates and picking the smallest fixes this.
